title: Aureline build-adapter descriptor, capability-flag, discovery-source, and precedence contract
description: Boundary schema for the adapter_descriptor_record every build-truth producer (native task runner, Build Server Protocol client, Bazel Build Event Protocol reader, structured importer, heuristic target inferrer, fallback opaque-preservation adapter) registers and every consumer (run / test / build / debug picker, quick-open, command palette, CI overlay, AI planner, support-bundle exporter) reads to know which adapters may publish target-graph snapshots and target descriptors with which confidence class, which capability flags, which discovery source, which workspace-scope compatibility, and which precedence class. Adapter rows in artifacts/tooling/adapter_map.yaml that publish target graphs project through this descriptor; the task-event adapter rows already registered there bind to schemas/tooling/task_event_envelope.schema.json. This schema does not mint a new confidence vocabulary, a new scope vocabulary, a new target-class vocabulary, or a new toolchain-class vocabulary; it re-exports the frozen tokens from schemas/tooling/task_event_envelope.schema.json (confidence_class) and schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json (scope_class, target_class, toolchain_class). Lower-confidence adapters MUST NOT silently replace higher-confidence adapters; replacements ride an adapter_replacement_event_record whose visible_state_change_class is non-null whenever the adapter swap reduces confidence. Adding a new adapter_class, capability_flag, discovery_source_class, workspace_scope_compatibility_class, precedence_class, replacement_posture, visible_state_change_class, or external_handoff_kind is additive-minor and bumps adapter_descriptor_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

adapter_descriptor_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the adapter-descriptor contract. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields and new enum members do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 UTC timestamp captured from a monotonic clock source.

opaque_id.description: Opaque stable identifier safe to log and export. Raw secrets, raw absolute paths, raw URLs, and raw payload bytes never appear here.

dotted_id.description: Dotted lower-snake id used for adapter_id, producer_tool, and discovery_source ids.

stable_token.description: Lowercase token id safe to embed in registry rows. Snake or kebab case.

short_sentence.description: Short reviewer-facing sentence safe to show in preview and export surfaces. Raw payload bytes, raw command lines, raw env bodies, and raw secret values never appear here.

adapter_class.description: Closed adapter-class vocabulary. native_build_runner is reserved for first-party native runners that own the build graph (cargo, go, npm-run-graph, etc.). build_server_protocol_client is reserved for BSP v2 servers that publish target graphs over the protocol. bazel_build_event_protocol is reserved for BEP-class streamed graphs (Bazel, Pants, Buck2-BEP). structured_importer is reserved for one-shot imports of a structured graph descriptor (CMake file API json, package.json / pnpm workspace, pyproject.toml + pdm/poetry export, etc.). heuristic_target_inferrer is reserved for filename / convention / regex-based discovery when no protocol or importer is available. fallback_opaque_preservation is the last-resort adapter that keeps an unknown source visible without claiming a typed graph.

confidence_class.description: Adapter confidence ceiling. Re-export of schemas/tooling/task_event_envelope.schema.json#/$defs/confidence_class. Pickers, CI overlays, and AI planners read this ceiling to choose which adapter's target graph wins when multiple adapters cover the same workspace.

capability_flag.description: Closed capability-flag vocabulary. preserves_target_id_across_adapter_swap means the adapter MAY publish a graph that re-uses target ids minted by another adapter under the same workspace identity (used during native -> structured-importer hand-off after a manifest change); adapters that cannot guarantee id stability across swap MUST omit this flag and an adapter_replacement_event_record for the swap MUST carry a visible_state_change_class.

discovery_source_class.description: Closed discovery-source vocabulary. ai_proposed_pending_review is reserved for graphs proposed by an AI tool call that have not yet been admitted; such adapters MUST set adapter_confidence_ceiling to heuristic_best_effort or lower and MUST set replacement_posture to never_replaces_existing_until_user_admits.

workspace_scope_compatibility_class.description: Re-export of schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json#/$defs/scope_class. Adapters declare which scope classes they can serve target-graph snapshots over; consumers asking for a scope an adapter cannot serve MUST treat the absence as not_observed_by_adapter rather than not_present_in_workspace.

precedence_class.description: Closed precedence-class vocabulary. When two adapters cover the same workspace under the same workset_scope, the higher precedence_class wins; the lower-precedence adapter's snapshot is downgraded to side-by-side advisory and MUST NOT replace the higher-precedence adapter's graph without an adapter_replacement_event_record carrying a visible_state_change_class.

replacement_posture.description: Closed replacement-posture vocabulary naming what the adapter is allowed to do when another adapter already owns the workset_scope. Higher-precedence adapters MAY use may_replace_lower_or_equal_precedence_silently; lower-precedence adapters MUST NOT. ai_proposed_pending_review discovery sources MUST use never_replaces_existing_until_user_admits.

visible_state_change_class.description: Closed visible-state-change vocabulary. no_visible_state_change_required is reserved for upgrades that strictly broaden the target set or strictly raise confidence and re-use existing target ids. Every other replacement MUST cite one of the disclosing classes so pickers, run/test/debug surfaces, CI overlays, and AI planners render a chip rather than swap silently.

external_handoff_kind.description: Closed external-handoff vocabulary used by the inspect-only fallback. Adapters that publish targets which can only be inspected (no run / test / build / debug action available locally) cite which external handoff is admissible so the picker renders an open-in-provider affordance rather than a generic Cannot run label.

capability_flag_set.description: Set of capability flags this adapter asserts. MUST include publishes_target_graph_snapshot if the adapter participates in the target-graph contract at all. heuristic_target_inferrer adapters MUST NOT include supports_run_action / supports_test_action / supports_build_action / supports_debug_action; those actions are owned by authoritative-source adapters. fallback_opaque_preservation adapters MUST include preserves_target_id_across_export_and_reopen and supports_inspect_only_external_handoff and MUST NOT include any supports_*_action flag.

workspace_scope_compatibility_set.description: Set of scope classes this adapter can serve target-graph snapshots over. An adapter that cannot serve sparse_slice MUST NOT silently down-fill a sparse_slice request to current_root; the consumer either re-asks under a covered scope or treats the absence as not_observed_by_adapter.

discovery_source_set.description: Set of discovery sources this adapter consumes. native_build_runner adapters typically consume manifest_files_in_workspace + workspace_lockfile; build_server_protocol_client consumes build_server_protocol_response; structured_importer consumes structured_graph_export_file; heuristic_target_inferrer consumes filename_or_directory_convention_heuristic and / or regex_or_glob_pattern_heuristic; fallback_opaque_preservation consumes opaque_unsupported_preservation.

supported_target_class_set.description: Re-export of schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json#/$defs/target_class. Names which execution-target classes the adapter can publish target descriptors against. Adapters MUST NOT mint new target classes.

supported_toolchain_class_set.description: Re-export of schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json#/$defs/toolchain_class. Names which toolchain classes the adapter expects in the active execution context. Adapters MUST NOT mint new toolchain classes.

adapter_id.description: Stable adapter id. Stable across producer-tool versions; adapter changes bump adapter_descriptor_schema_version (for breaking payload changes) or the adapter_version field below (for non-breaking version drift).

producer_tool.description: Dotted id of the originating tool (for example cargo.metadata, bsp.server, bazel.build_event_stream, cmake.file_api, heuristic.target_inferrer).

adapter_version.description: Opaque adapter-side version string. For heuristic adapters this is typically a ruleset id (for example heuristic.target_inferrer.ruleset_2026_04); for protocol clients this is the protocol revision the client speaks.

task_event_adapter_id_refs.description: Refs to task-event adapter rows in artifacts/tooling/adapter_map.yaml whose task / build / test / debug envelopes resolve back to targets this adapter publishes. May be empty for adapters that publish target graphs but not task events (for example, a one-shot structured importer); MUST be non-empty for native_build_runner adapters.

external_handoff_kinds_supported.description: External-handoff kinds the adapter can offer when a target reaches inspect_only_no_action. Empty when supports_inspect_only_external_handoff is not in capability_flag_set; non-empty (with at least one kind other than no_handoff_admissible) when it is.

graph_freshness_default_class.description: Default freshness class the adapter publishes graph snapshots under. Individual snapshots MAY narrow further. Null when the adapter does not publish snapshots (capability_flag_set excludes publishes_target_graph_snapshot).

$schema.description: Optional schema pointer used only by fixture files so editors can resolve the boundary schema; never part of the runtime record.

__fixture__.description: Optional fixture annotation block; never part of the runtime record.

adapter_descriptor_record.description: One adapter descriptor row. Build pickers, quick-open, run / test / build / debug surfaces, CI overlays, and AI planners read these descriptors to decide which adapter's target graph and target descriptors to render and which adapter's swap MUST disclose a visible state change.

target_id_continuity.description: Names whether target ids minted by the previous adapter survive the swap. all_target_ids_preserved is reserved for adapters that carry preserves_target_id_across_adapter_swap. subset_of_target_ids_preserved and no_target_ids_preserved_review_required force the swap to render a target_set_narrowed_with_review_chip / target_set_widened_with_review_chip / discovery_source_changed_with_review_chip visible state change.

adapter_replacement_event_record.description: Records one adapter swap. Pickers, run / test / build / debug surfaces, CI overlays, and AI planners cite this record to render the visible state change rather than rotate adapters silently.

allOf.description: heuristic_target_inferrer adapters MUST NOT claim authoritative_from_source or structured_parse_match confidence.
structured_importer adapters MAY claim structured_parse_match but MUST NOT claim authoritative_from_source.
fallback_opaque_preservation adapters MUST set adapter_confidence_ceiling = unknown and precedence_class = fallback_opaque_preservation_only.
ai_proposed_pending_review discovery_source MUST pair with replacement_posture = never_replaces_existing_until_user_admits and confidence at most heuristic_best_effort.
Adapters that include supports_inspect_only_external_handoff in capability_flag_set MUST declare at least one external_handoff_kind other than no_handoff_admissible.
Replacement events whose new adapter has lower precedence than the previous adapter MUST carry a non-trivial visible_state_change_class (not no_visible_state_change_required).
Replacement events with target_id_continuity != all_target_ids_preserved MUST carry a non-trivial visible_state_change_class so target ids never silently rename.
ai_proposed_pending_user_admission replacements MUST NOT swap the active adapter; they coexist as a proposal.
